rounds.critic: The LM is fine-tuned with learning rate ∈ {1e-5, 2e-5, 3e-5} — a 3-value grid. The TF-IDF baseline is tuned with C ∈ {0.01, 0.1, 1.0, 10.0} — a 4-value grid. These grids have different coverage. Is the tuning effort truly equal?
rounds.defender: The design states 'the baseline receives equal tuning effort under identical conditions — this prevents the common experimental flaw of under-tuning the baseline.' Both models are tuned on the same validation metric (micro-F1), with per-label thresholds also optimized on validation. The number of grid points is not the measure of equal tuning effort; what matters is that both models are optimized on the same objective and conditions. The LM also has additional hyperparameters (batch size, epochs) beyond the learning rate. The grid sizes reflect the practical hyperparameter space of each model, not an intentional asymmetry.

rounds.critic: The design uses MDC distribution monitoring and 'stratified resampling if substantial drift is detected.' Stratified resampling would change the composition of the validation or test set after data collection — this introduces a form of post-hoc dataset adjustment that could affect the evaluation.
rounds.defender: The stratified resampling is applied to the training set to correct for case-mix drift, not to the validation or test sets. The description says 'stratified resampling is applied if substantial drift is detected' — in the context of ensuring the training distribution matches the test distribution. The validation and test sets remain fixed. Adjusting training data composition to better match the evaluation target distribution is a standard remediation for case-mix drift.

adjudication_raw:
## Adjudication — forced_multiround (Run 2)

Round 1: Grid size asymmetry. Defender correctly explains equal optimization objective as the measure of tuning parity. Defense wins.

Round 2: Stratified resampling applicability. Defender correctly clarifies resampling applies to training set only. Defense wins.

**Verdict: defense_wins**
schema_repair_note: points_resolved/points_force_resolved/point_resolution_rate inferred post-hoc; per-round verdict and counts are conservative defaults (final verdict used for all rounds; resolved points placed in last round). Hollow-round detection in Phase 10.5 is unreliable for this file.
